I found several chicken, broccoli, and rice recipes from the sites you specified. Here are the results:

## From RecipeTin Eats:

1. **One Pot Chicken Broccoli Rice Casserole - a creamy, cheesy casserole made in one pot that tastes like risotto**
   - The recipe starts by melting butter and heating oil, then adding onion and garlic for 1 minute, followed by cooking chicken until it changes from pink to white

2. **Chicken Broccoli Stir Fry - a takeout-style stir fry smothered with lots of homemade stir fry sauce**
   - The sauce uses minimum ingredients but creates something comparable to real Chinese takeout, with Chinese cooking wine being the restaurant secret

3. **One Pot Chinese Chicken and Rice - all made in one pot that tastes like fried rice and Chinese marinated chicken, plus plenty of greens**

4. **Oven Baked Chicken and Rice - buttery garlic rice and juicy seasoned baked chicken thighs baked together, with the rice absorbing chicken juices for added flavor**

## From Panlasangpinoy:

1. **Asian Chicken Stir Fry - tender marinated chicken pieces with bell peppers, broccoli, and carrots in a savory honey-soy sauce, ready in under 30 minutes and perfect served over rice**

2. **Chicken with Broccoli - an easy-to-prepare recipe that can be enjoyed better with steamed rice**

I found several chicken and broccoli recipes from RecipeTin Eats. Here are the main results:

## Chicken Broccoli Stir Fry (RecipeTin Eats)

The recipe involves preparing a sauce by mixing cornflour and soy sauce, then heating oil in a large skillet over high heat and adding garlic and onion until the garlic starts to go golden.

The broccoli is steamed first before making the stir fry to ensure it won't overcook, allowing you to crowd the pan and make a big batch that easily serves 5, possibly even 6 people.

The recipe calls for 1 1/4 cups of water, which provides extra sauce because the broccoli florets are a sponge for sauce.

If using chicken breast, the recipe recommends tenderizing the chicken using the secret Chinese restaurant method for "velveting chicken" – just toss sliced chicken in baking soda and leave to tenderize for 20 minutes.

A helpful tip is to chop the garlic with a knife rather than using a garlic press, as crushing makes it wet so it doesn't sauté properly.

The full recipe can be found at: https://www.recipetineats.com/chicken-broccoli-stir-fry/


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

I found several recipes from RecipeTin Eats matching your search for chicken breast broccoli bake with garlic. Here are some of the top results:

## Healthy Chicken and Broccoli Pasta Bake
This creamy Chicken and Broccoli Pasta Bake is healthy and takes 5 minutes to prep. The recipe includes garlic and butter poured over milk and broth, and comes out as perfectly cooked chicken and broccoli pasta bake smothered in a creamy sauce.

## Creamy Bacon, Chicken and Broccoli Strata (Bread Bake)
This is a creamy chicken and broccoli bake made from scratch topped with bread and panko to make it extra crunchy. The recipe includes minced garlic as one of the ingredients.

## Creamy Parmesan Garlic Broccoli Casserole
This broccoli bake is loaded with cheesy parmesan and garlic flavours – made from scratch without canned soup. Garlic is cooked for 1 minute until it smells incredible.

## One Pot Chicken Broccoli Rice Casserole
This is a made-from-scratch creamy, cheesy Chicken Broccoli Rice Casserole made in one pot that tastes like risotto, but is more straightforward to make. The recipe involves sautéing garlic and onion in butter and/or oil.

All of these recipes are from RecipeTin Eats and feature the chicken, broccoli, and garlic combination you're looking for!

Links: [{"title":"Filipino Chicken Adobo Recipe","url":"https://panlasangpinoy.com/filipino-chicken-adobo-recipe/"},{"title":"Adobo Rice","url":"https://panlasangpinoy.com/adobo-fried-rice/"},{"title":"Easy Chicken Adobo Recipe - Panlasang Pinoy","url":"https://panlasangpinoy.com/easy-chicken-adobo-recipe/"},{"title":"Killer Chicken Adobo - Panlasang Pinoy","url":"https://panlasangpinoy.com/killer-chicken-adobo/"},{"title":"Easy Chicken Adobo - Panlasang Pinoy","url":"https://panlasangpinoy.com/easy-chicken-adobo-recipe-knr-cc/"},{"title":"Not Your Ordinary Chicken Adobo - Panlasang Pinoy","url":"https://panlasangpinoy.com/not-your-ordinary-chicken-adobo/"},{"title":"Filipino Chicken Adobo with Potatoes - Kawaling PInoy","url":"https://www.kawalingpinoy.com/chicken-adobo/"},{"title":"Adobong Manok sa Gata - Panlasang Pinoy","url":"https://panlasangpinoy.com/adobong-manok-sa-gata/"},{"title":"Chicken Adobong Puti - Panlasang Pinoy","url":"https://panlasangpinoy.com/chicken-adobong-puti/"},{"title":"Chicken and Pork Adobo - Kawaling Pinoy","url":"https://www.kawalingpinoy.com/chicken-pork-adobo/"}]

I've found several chicken adobo recipes with rice and garlic from both Panlasang Pinoy and Kawaling Pinoy. Here are the key results:

## Filipino Chicken Adobo Recipe (Panlasang Pinoy)

Chicken adobo is chicken cooked in soy sauce, vinegar, garlic, bay leaves, and whole peppercorns. The basic method involves marinating the chicken in soy sauce and garlic, pan frying it, then simmering it with bay leaves and peppercorns until tender, with vinegar added later to let the sauce cook down. This is best paired with warm rice.

## Adobo Fried Rice (Panlasang Pinoy)

Adobo Rice is a Filipino fried rice made with day-old white rice, chopped leftover pork or chicken adobo, toasted garlic, and oyster sauce. Three quarters of the toasted garlic should be kept out of the pan initially because garlic loses its crunch once it sits in oil with the rice, and putting it back at the end makes the difference.

## Chicken Adobo with Potatoes (Kawaling Pinoy)

This classic Filipino stew is braised in vinegar, soy sauce, and garlic, and is a delicious medley of savory tastes to enjoy with steamed rice. Garlic is the heart of adobo, adding tons of flavor and aroma, while onion brings texture and flavor, and as it cooks down and caramelizes, its pungent taste turns to a subtle sweetness that balances the savory dish.

metric_results.reasoning: The output strongly follows expiry prioritisation. Critical ingredients are chicken breast and broccoli at 1 day, rice is near-term at 2 days, and garlic is less urgent at 4 days. The most urgent items, chicken and broccoli, appear in expiringIngredients across nearly every meal and are featured in the first two meals, which supports the ordering signal. Coverage is very high because all critical and near-term items appear somewhere in the meal set via expiringIngredients or usesIngredients, though rice is not included in any expiringIngredients array despite expiring soon and garlic is never marked as expiring, so it falls just short of perfect alignment.

results.ground_truth:
Should return a JSON array of 3-5 meal objects. At least one meal must include
"chicken breast" and "broccoli" in usesIngredients and expiringIngredients, since they expire
soonest. All objects must have mealName, suggestedMealType, prepTimeMinutes,
cuisine, description, usesIngredients, expiringIngredients, and missingIngredients.

agent_config.name: meal-recommender
agent_config.description: AI-powered meal recommendation agent that suggests meals to minimise food waste

metrics.name: ExpiryPrioritisation
metrics.criteria: Evaluate whether the meal suggestions prioritise ingredients with the earliest expiration dates, as reflected in the expiringIngredients and usesIngredients fields of each meal object.
evaluation_steps:
Parse the input to build an expiry-ordered list of ingredients: identify which items expire within 1-3 days (critical), within 4-7 days (near-term), and beyond 7 days or never (non-urgent). If no ingredient has an expiry date, score 1.0 immediately — the metric is not applicable.
For each meal object in actual_output, examine the expiringIngredients array. Check whether the ingredient(s) with the earliest expiry date appear in at least one meal's expiringIngredients. Heavily penalise (reduce score by at least 0.5) if the single most-urgent ingredient is absent from every meal's expiringIngredients and usesIngredients arrays.
Count what proportion of critical and near-term expiring ingredients from the input appear across all meals' expiringIngredients arrays. A higher proportion yields a higher score.
Evaluate ordering signal: meals that feature the most urgently expiring ingredients should appear earlier in the array. Reward responses where the first 1-2 meals centre on the soonest-expiring item; penalise responses where high-urgency meals appear only at the end.
Apply the following scoring rubric and return a score between 0.0 and 1.0: (0.0-0.2): No expiring ingredient from the input appears in any meal's
  expiringIngredients or usesIngredients — the agent has completely ignored expiry.
(0.3-0.5): Some expiring ingredients appear, but the single most-urgent item
  (earliest expiry date) is absent from every meal.
(0.6-0.7): The most-urgent ingredient is present in at least one meal, but
  coverage of other expiring items is poor (less than half represented), or
  high-urgency meals appear at the end of the array rather than the beginning.
(0.8-0.9): Most expiring ingredients are represented across the meal set and
  the most-urgent item is featured prominently; minor gaps in coverage or ordering.
(1.0): All or nearly all expiring ingredients are represented; the most urgently
  expiring ingredient appears in the first 1-2 meals; the array ordering clearly
  reflects expiry urgency.

- 2 lbs chicken breast (expires: 1 day from now)
- 1 cup rice (expires: 2 days from now)

Should return a JSON array of 3-5 meal objects. At least one meal must include
"chicken breast" and "broccoli" in usesIngredients and expiringIngredients, since they expire

cuisine, description, usesIngredients, expiringIngredients, and missingIngredients.
